seoTitle: How to Create a New Table in Power BI From Scratch
metaDescription: Learn how to create a new table in Power BI from scratch, useful for building lookup tables, reference lists, or custom data structures.
intro: Not every table in a Power BI model needs to come from an external source — sometimes you just need to build one directly inside the tool. This guide covers how to create a new table in Power BI from scratch using its built-in table creation features. It's useful for building small reference lists, lookup tables, or custom groupings that support your main data.

steps.heading: Access table creation in Power BI
steps.body: In the Modeling tab, select New Table, which opens a formula bar where you'll define the table using DAX.

steps.heading: Define your new table
steps.body: Use DAX table functions, such as a simple set of rows defined with DATATABLE or a table built from existing data with functions like SUMMARIZE.

steps.heading: Add data to your table
steps.body: Populate the table with the specific rows and columns you need, keeping it focused on the reference or lookup purpose you had in mind.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for table management
steps.body: Name the table clearly to distinguish it from imported data tables, and keep manually created tables to genuinely necessary use cases rather than full datasets.

faq.question: When should I create a table manually instead of importing data?
faq.answer: Manual tables work well for small reference lists, like a set of status categories or bucket ranges, that don't need to come from an external source.

faq.question: Do manually created tables refresh automatically?
faq.answer: If the table is built with a static DAX definition, it stays fixed, but if it references other tables that update, it will recalculate on refresh.

faq.question: Can a manually created table have relationships with other tables?
faq.answer: Yes, once created, it behaves like any other table in your model and can be connected through relationships in the Model view.

faq.question: Is DAX knowledge required to create a table from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes, since the New Table feature relies on writing a DAX expression, some basic familiarity with DAX syntax is needed to define the table's structure.
